title: Mild Crispy Norwegian Lamb Gratin
description: A savory, crispy, one-pot Norwegian dish featuring liquid cheese, beurre-monte, and anti-inflammatory-friendly ingredients.
instructions: Season the lamb shoulder with salt, black pepper, and caraway seeds.
Heat 1 tablespoon of beurre-monte in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the lamb shoulder until browned on all sides, about 2 minutes per side.
Remove the lamb from the pot and set aside.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of beurre-monte to the pot, then add the sliced onion and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the flour to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Gradually add the mascarpone cheese, whisking constantly to avoid lumps.
Return the lamb shoulder to the pot and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the lamb is tender and the top is golden brown.
Remove the pot from the oven and garnish with fresh parsley, thyme, and a sprinkle of salt and pepper.
